quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Ion Dissociation and Precipitation Reactions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Ion Dissociation, Moles of Ions from Ionic Compounds, Precipitation Reactions, Molarity and Volume Calculations

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced by the dissociation of 1 mol of Al₂(CO₃)₃?
questions.explanation:
Al₂(CO₃)₃ → 2Al³⁺ + 3CO₃²⁻
Total ions = 2 + 3 = 5 moles of ions per mole of compound

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced by the dissociation of 1 mol of Na₂SO₄?
questions.explanation:
Na₂SO₄ → 2Na⁺ + SO₄²⁻
Total ions = 2 + 1 = 3 moles of ions per mole of compound

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced by the dissociation of 1 mol of (NH₄)₂Cr₂O₇ (Ammonium Dichromate)?
questions.explanation:
(NH₄)₂Cr₂O₇ → 2NH₄⁺ + Cr₂O₇²⁻
Total ions = 2 + 1 = 3 moles of ions per mole of compound

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced by the dissociation of 0.4 moles of FeCl₃?
questions.explanation:
FeCl₃ → Fe³⁺ + 3Cl⁻
Ions per mole = 1 + 3 = 4 moles of ions per mole of FeCl₃
For 0.4 moles: 0.4 × 4 = 1.6 moles of ions

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced by the dissociation of 1 mol of Ca(NO₃)₂?
questions.explanation:
Ca(NO₃)₂ → Ca²⁺ + 2NO₃⁻
Total ions = 1 + 2 = 3 moles of ions per mole of compound

questions.question: How many moles of ions are produced when 1 mol of AgCl is placed in water?
questions.explanation: AgCl is insoluble in water (very low Ksp), so it does not dissociate significantly. Practically 0 moles of ions are produced.

questions.question: If you have 3.0 moles of H₂SO₄ and want to create a highly concentrated 18.0 M solution, what is the maximum volume you can produce?
questions.explanation: Volume = moles / M = 3.0 mol / 18.0 M = 0.167 L

questions.question: A chemist needs a 2.0 M solution of MgCl₂ that contains exactly 0.10 moles of the salt. What is the required volume in milliliters (mL)?
questions.explanation:
Volume in L = 0.10 mol / 2.0 M = 0.05 L
Volume in mL = 0.05 × 1000 = 50 mL

questions.question: When two ionic compounds are combined and a solid forms, the process is called __________
questions.explanation: Precipitation is the formation of an insoluble solid when two solutions are mixed. Hydration is the attraction of water molecules to ions, dissociation is the separation of ions, and crystallization is the formation of solid crystals from a solution.

questions.question: Which of the following is the right side of the equation for dissolving the compound K₂S (s)?
questions.explanation: K₂S dissociates into 2K⁺ ions and one S²⁻ ion when dissolved in water: K₂S(s) → 2K⁺(aq) + S²⁻(aq)

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered ion dissociation and precipitation reactions! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the dissociation equations for different ionic compounds.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on counting the number of ions produced per formula unit.
scoreMessages.0: Please review how ionic compounds dissociate in water and how to count the total ions produced.
